mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and higher equity valuation multiples. When the policy rate is declining, S&P 500 forward returns should be positive over the next month because the market prices a more supportive liquidity regime.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or the sign consistency is below 55% during periods when FEDFUNDS has trended down for at least 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above its recent pace implies inflation pressure that raises the odds of tighter policy and higher real discount rates. That should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once inflation momentum crosses a meaningful threshold.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average by 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL acceleration is above the stated threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and corporate revenue outlook. Under that joint stress, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no predictive value over either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of months with rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO signals stronger industrial supply-side momentum and firmer real activity, which supports cyclical earnings and near-term equity performance. A sustained uptrend in industrial production should therefore produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO do not yield positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if rank correlation between INDPRO change and forward SPX returns is near zero for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger consumer demand and better revenue realization for large-cap firms. When retail sales growth accelerates beyond a threshold, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 5 trading days as the market prices stronger current-quarter sales.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered retail-sales expansions are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect vanishes after two consecutive positive RSAFS prints.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent upswing in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad final-demand growth, not just inventory noise. That joint demand confirmation should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings expectations and sales breadth improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint-expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold marks a deterioration in labor income and risk appetite that should pressure equities quickly. Once unemployment rises materially, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days should be negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing unemployment shocks are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the signal is not stronger than random after the threshold event.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while HOUST remains weak, the economy shows industrial resilience but housing-sector softness, a combination that can hold back aggregate growth and risk sentiment. This mixed supply-demand pattern should be negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in a tightening-credit backdrop.
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-HOUST divergence does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips to positive during periods without elevated FEDFUNDS.
